methodology: Offline beta replay over open-source-style LLM app reductions and one explicitly synthetic abstention-message fixture. Each case runs a real PromptABI config, checks expected and tuned-absent rule IDs, records upstreamable issues, and stores concise case-study notes without copying upstream source.

issues.title: User text can render as assistant control delimiter in ChatML-style templates

tuning_actions: Kept the finding scoped to structural delimiter forgery rather than semantic prompt-injection claims.
Added tuned-absent role-boundary-abstained coverage so unsupported-template regressions are not mistaken for proved bugs.

case_study.summary: A role delimiter supplied inside user-controlled content becomes indistinguishable from model control structure after rendering.
case_study.root_cause: The template concatenates trusted role markers and unescaped user content in the same byte language.
case_study.production_symptom: A downstream model or parser may receive a prompt containing a forged assistant prefix.
case_study.fix: Escape, JSON-encode, or otherwise wrap user-controlled fields before placing them beside role delimiters.

issues.title: Application parser accepts ticket states outside the constrained schema

tuning_actions: Recorded parser-compatibility-abstained as an absent rule to keep mismatch evidence decisive.
Preserved the minimized ticket schema as a case study instead of relying on prose-only bug claims.

case_study.summary: A structured-output parser accepts strings that the declared schema excludes.
case_study.root_cause: The application parser and constrained-decoding schema encode different languages.
case_study.production_symptom: CI can pass schema generation while runtime accepts malformed or semantically invalid tickets.
case_study.fix: Align parser validation with the exact schema language or narrow the runtime parser.

issues.title: Tool argument serialization drifts from provider envelope assumptions

tuning_actions: Separated schema ingestion success from serialization warnings so valid schemas do not hide provider-envelope bugs.

case_study.summary: Tool schemas load correctly, but the provider/template/parser path serializes arguments incompatibly.
case_study.root_cause: Provider envelope fields and runtime parser assumptions disagree on argument encoding and IDs.
case_study.production_symptom: The app receives a tool call whose name or arguments cannot be routed reliably.
case_study.fix: Pin a provider envelope contract and replay representative serialized tool calls before deployment.

issues.title: RAG chunk metadata and template overhead drop required citations

tuning_actions: Added citation-loss and overlap-accounting expectations to avoid counting generic budget warnings as the case-study bug.

case_study.summary: Retrieved chunks fit in isolation but lose citations once metadata and prompt-template overhead are counted.
case_study.root_cause: The retrieval tokenizer and prompt-budget tokenizer account for chunk boundaries differently.
case_study.production_symptom: The model sees answer text without the citation field required by the application.
case_study.fix: Verify retrieval payloads with the serving tokenizer and mark citations as must-survive prompt regions.

issues.title: Provider migration loses tool IDs, streaming chunks, and structured-output fields

tuning_actions: Grouped provider findings by migration edge so a single incompatible target does not inflate false-positive counts.

case_study.summary: A nominally OpenAI-compatible target changes tool-call and streaming semantics.
case_study.root_cause: The target provider omits or renames fields that the source-side application treats as stable.
case_study.production_symptom: Streaming reconstruction, tool routing, or structured-output validation breaks after migration.
case_study.fix: Replay source and target provider fixtures and gate migration on compatible envelope fields.

issues.title: SFT target roles include a role absent from the serving chat template

tuning_actions: Kept training target-role alignment as a static-contract violation so unsupported solver fragments remain visible as abstentions.

case_study.summary: The data builder can place supervised target spans outside the intended assistant role.
case_study.root_cause: Training manifest target roles drift from the serving chat-template role set.
case_study.production_symptom: Fine-tuning may train on user or tool text as if it were assistant output.
case_study.fix: Pin the training tokenizer/template pair and prove target masks align with rendered assistant regions.

tuning_actions: Requires abstentions to carry a witness and concrete setup suggestion instead of silently passing as unknown.
Labels this case as synthetic so beta evidence does not overclaim real-world provenance.

case_study.summary: A malformed tokenizer metadata field prevents stop-tokenizer analysis from making a proof claim.
case_study.root_cause: The tokenizer backend cannot be constructed because normalization metadata is not a supported string or list.
case_study.production_symptom: CI reports an explicit abstention rather than pretending stop-policy/tokenizer compatibility was checked.
case_study.fix: Correct the tokenizer metadata or switch to a supported local tokenizer artifact before relying on the stop analysis.
